title: toddalia venosa
intro: Toddalia venosa, also known as the poisonous dodder, is a type of plant found in New Zealand and parts of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the digestive system and relieve indigestion. The health benefits of Toddalia venosa include its antispasmodic and carminative properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal issues. Toddalia venosa is typically prepared as a tincture or tea, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of digestive discomfort.
